children.name: Steatornis
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the remarkable oilbird, the only nocturnal, fruit-eating bird that navigates by echolocation. It is one of the most unusual birds in the world, with evolutionary affinities still debated.

children.name: Steatornis caripensis
children.description: The oilbird, locally known as the guácharo, is a bird species found in the northern areas of South America including the Caribbean island of Trinidad. It is the only living species in the genus Steatornis, the family Steatornithidae, and the order Steatornithiformes. Nesting in colonies in caves, oilbirds are nocturnal feeders on the fruits of the oil palm and tropical laurels.
